input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
                    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

        <!-- Pull-to-refresh via wrapping in RefreshView -->
        <CollectionView.Parent>
            <!-- see below -->
        </CollectionView.Parent>

        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate>
                <VerticalStackLayout Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="18" />
                    <Label Text="{Binding Description}" FontSize="14"
                           TextColor="Gray" />
                </VerticalStackLayout>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</ContentPage>
```

Since `CollectionView` doesn't have a built-in refresh, wrap it in a `RefreshView`:

```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
                    SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding ItemSelectedCommand}"
                    SelectionChangedCommandParameter="{Binding SelectedItem}">

        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate>
                <VerticalStackLayout Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="18" />
                    <Label Text="{Binding Description}"
                           FontSize="14" TextColor="Gray" />
                </VerticalStackLayout>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel** (using `CommunityToolkit.Mvvm`):

```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;

    [ObservableProperty]
    ObservableCollection<Item> items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    Item? selectedItem;

    [ObservableProperty]
    bool isRefreshing;

    public ItemsViewModel(IApiService api)
    {
        _api = api;
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    async Task Refresh()
    {
        try
        {
            var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
            Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(data);
        }
        finally
        {
            IsRefreshing = false;   // stops the spinner
        }
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    async Task ItemSelected()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;

        await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(
            $"{nameof(DetailPage)}",
            new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Item"] = SelectedItem });

        SelectedItem = null;        // deselect so they can re-tap
    }
}
```

### Key binding mechanics

| Binding | Direction | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"` | One-way | Feeds the list |
| `SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"` | Two-way | VM reads the tap **and** clears it after navigation |
| `IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"` | Two-way | VM sets `false` to dismiss the spinner |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | One-way | Fires `ItemSelectedCommand` on tap |
| `RefreshView.Command` | One-way | Fires `RefreshCommand` on pull |

### Things to watch out for

1. **Set `SelectedItem = null`** after navigating — otherwise tapping the same row a second time won't fire `SelectionChanged`.
2. **Set `IsRefreshing = false`** in a `finally` block so the spinner always stops, even on errors.
3. **`SelectionChangedCommand`** was added in .NET MAUI; if you're on Xamarin.Forms, handle the `SelectionChanged` event and call into the VM instead.
4. Register the route in `AppShell.xaml.cs`:
   ```csharp
   Routing.RegisterRoute(nameof(DetailPage), typeof(DetailPage));
   ```
